question: Has the SCF already funded any price-oracle projects? I want to avoid duplicating something that exists.

golden.answer: Yes. Official SCF records confirm substantial prior oracle work as of **2026-07-10**: **Reflector $444,840 awarded/paid** across four awarded submissions; **Band Protocol $60,000 awarded/paid in SCF #16**; **Lightecho $93,110 awarded/paid**; **Orally $48,000 awarded/paid** (a later $102K row is not awarded); and **DIA Oracles $38,000 awarded/paid**. Additional examples include **Chainlink Oracles Relayer $38,400 awarded/paid** and **Stellar Oracle Shield $120,000 awarded / $12,000 paid**. Scout has a conflicting Band alias row showing $100K/#41; the official Band project record controls, so do not present that as an equally supported amount. Use this history to explain differentiation, not to declare a new proposal automatically disqualified.
keyFacts: Returns named prior oracle awards from status-bearing official records.
Uses Band's official $60K/#16 result and discloses the service alias conflict.
avoid: Do NOT call Band a $100K awarded project or infer awarded/paid status from a bare budget field.
Do NOT treat overlapping prior work as automatic disqualification.
golden.notes:
GT-37 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: official Band is $60K awarded/paid in #16; the Scout $100K/#41 alias is contradicted service evidence. Require official status/paid provenance for amounts and preserve differentiation framing.
GT-37 independently confirmed by processes 3296/3298.

corroboration.claim: Official status-bearing records confirm funded oracle prior art and establish Band at $60K, not the conflicting Scout $100K alias
